attacking_style: Pure sprinter who launches from technical/chaotic finishes rather than textbook leadouts. Generates high momentum from behind but timing remains inconsistent—needs precise final 150m positioning.
team_tactics: Receives full leadout investment in Grand Tours with patient three-week development. Team willing to redirect resources from veteran sprinters (SAGAN precedent) once commitment made to MEEUS as protected rider.
terrain_preference: Technical flat finishes where positioning chaos favors late acceleration. Vulnerable on cobbled classics—crashes early before secteurs determine selection. Pure pancake sprints show consistent top-5 capability.
vulnerability: Early-race crashes eliminate him before key terrain (Paris-Roubaix example). Late launch timing costs wins despite superior speed—multiple photo finishes lost when coming from behind with momentum.
key_patterns: Photo finish specialist: lost to VAN AERT, beat PHILIPSEN by inches—elite speed unmatched timing (2 instances)
Three-week Grand Tour investment pays dividends: team builds leadout confidence progressively until final week (Tour 2023)
Coming-from-behind momentum strategy backfires: faster sprinting can't overcome positional deficit in tight finishes (2 losses)
Progression trajectory visible: 4th Vuelta 2021 → matching VAN AERT 2022 → Champs-Élysées win 2023
form_trajectory: positive: Won Champs-Élysées in 2023 Tour debut after two-year progression from 4th places to beating elite sprinters, though Roubaix crash shows classics vulnerability persists
